devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्थं सोऽनुग्रहीतोऽङ्ग कृष्णेनेक्ष्वाकु नन्दनः । तं परिक्रम्य सन्नम्य निश्चक्राम गुहामुखात् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca itthaṃ so taṃ parikramya sannamya niścakrāma guhāmukhāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: You are the glorious consort of goddess Lakṣmī, loved by Your devotees, the Supreme Controller cf the Universe who are resorted to for protection by helpless people and as such also by me. Taking this into account, be pleased to grant the prayer of mine who am a servant of your lotus-like feet. Oh dearest Lord I have submitted myself to your feet.

devanagari: संवीक्ष्य क्षुल्लकान्मर्त्यान्पशून्वीरुद्वनस्पतीन् । मत्वा कलियुगं प्राप्तं जगाम दिशमुत्तराम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃvīkṣya kṣullakānmartyānpaśūnvīrudvanaspatīn matvā kaliyugaṃ prāptaṃ jagāma diśamuttarām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Placing the letter (written by Rukmiṇī) at his feet, he bowed down to him. The Lord of the world, with beautiful smiling eyes, took it up. The Lord, Dharma incarnate, read it attentively as if he was eagerly desirous to read it.

devanagari: तपःश्रद्धायुतो धीरो निःसङ्गो मुक्तसंशयः । समाधाय मनः कृष्णे प्राविशद्गन्धमादनम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tapaḥśraddhāyuto dhīro niḥsaṅgo muktasaṃśayaḥ samādhāya manaḥ kṛṣṇe prāviśadgandhamādanam
translation: Climbing up mount Gomanta which was inhabited by Siddhas, Cāraṇas, Gandharvas and Vidyādharas, Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa hastily ascended its peak known as Pravarṣaṇa as clouds always shower rains there. They happily lived there subsisting on fruits and roots, Oh king.

devanagari: बदर्याश्रममासाद्य नरनारायणालयम् । सर्वद्वन्द्वसहः शान्तस्तपसाराधयद्धरिम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: badaryāśramamāsādya naranārāyaṇālayam sarvadvandvasahaḥ śāntastapasārādhayaddharim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: Feeling elated and happy, Jarāsandha also pursued the two Mādhava brothers (Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) and laid siege to the Gomanta mountain, surrounding it with bis vast army.

devanagari: भगवान्पुनराव्रज्य पुरीं यवनवेष्टिताम् । हत्वा म्लेच्छबलं निन्ये तदीयं द्वारकां धनम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavānpunarāvrajya purīṃ yavanaveṣṭitām hatvā mlecchabalaṃ ninye tadīyaṃ dvārakāṃ dhanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ veṣṭ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ veṣṭ ]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: He summoned his followers, the allied princes proud and irresistible in ṅght, such as (kings of) Kaliṅga, Kuñjarapati, the ruler of Śurāṣṭra (Kathiawar), Jayadratha the king of Sindhu (lower Sindh), Somadatta of the Kuru family, Śakuni the king of Gandhāra and Rukmi, the (crown) prince of Vidarbha, Sudakṣiṇa of Kāmboja and Virāṭa, the king of Matsyas. He commanded them to go up and nght from the east of Gomanta.

devanagari: नीयमाने धने गोभिर्नृभिश्चाच्युतचोदितैः । आजगाम जरासन्धस्त्रयोविंशत्यनीकपः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: nīyamāne dhane gobhirnṛbhiścācyutacoditaiḥ ājagāma jarāsandhastrayoviṃśatyanīkapaḥ

translation: He summoned his followers, the allied princes proud and irresistible in ṅght, such as (kings of) Kaliṅga, Kuñjarapati, the ruler of Śurāṣṭra (Kathiawar), Jayadratha the king of Sindhu (lower Sindh), Somadatta of the Kuru family, Śakuni the king of Gandhāra and Rukmi, the (crown) prince of Vidarbha, Sudakṣiṇa of Kāmboja and Virāṭa, the king of Matsyas. He commanded them to go up and nght from the east of Gomanta.

devanagari: विलोक्य वेगरभसं रिपुसैन्यस्य माधवौ । मनुष्यचेष्टामापन्नौ राजन्दुद्रुवतुर्द्रुतम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: vilokya vegarabhasaṃ ripusainyasya mādhavau manuṣyaceṣṭāmāpannau rājandudruvaturdrutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: He summoned his followers, the allied princes proud and irresistible in ṅght, such as (kings of) Kaliṅga, Kuñjarapati, the ruler of Śurāṣṭra (Kathiawar), Jayadratha the king of Sindhu (lower Sindh), Somadatta of the Kuru family, Śakuni the king of Gandhāra and Rukmi, the (crown) prince of Vidarbha, Sudakṣiṇa of Kāmboja and Virāṭa, the king of Matsyas. He commanded them to go up and nght from the east of Gomanta.

devanagari: विहाय वित्तं प्रचुरमभीतौ भीरुभीतवत् । पद्भ्यां पलाशाभ्यां चेलतुर्बहुयोजनम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: vihāya vittaṃ pracuramabhītau bhīrubhītavat padbhyāṃ palāśābhyāṃ celaturbahuyojanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vid class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 7 verb]

translation: For scaling (and attacking) the mountain Gomanta from its southern side, Jarāsandha ordered the following kings—Drupada, the king of Pāñcālas, Damaghoṣa, the ruler of Cedis, Brahmadatta, king of Śālvas and Ekalavya, the chief of Niṣādas, Bāhlika, the foremost of Kurus and the unconquerable Yudhāmanyu, Vinda and Anuvinda of Avanti and Pauṇḍraka, the king of Kāśi.

devanagari: पलायमानौ तौ दृष्ट्वा मागधः प्रहसन्बली । अन्वधावद्रथानीकैरीशयोरप्रमाणवित् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: palāyamānau tau dṛṣṭvā māgadhaḥ prahasanbalī anvadhāvadrathānīkairīśayorapramāṇavit

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative dual from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative dual from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative dual from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Having thus climbed mount Gomanta abounding in fruitbearing trees of all seasons, they searched for Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma everywhere below the peak Pravarṣaṇa. Not finding Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa, they deforested the mount cutting down all the trees.

devanagari: प्रद्रुत्य दूरं संश्रान्तौ तुङ्गमारुहतां गिरिम् । प्रवर्षणाख्यं भगवान्नित्यदा यत्र वर्षति ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: pradrutya dūraṃ saṃśrāntau tuṅgamāruhatāṃ girim pravarṣaṇākhyaṃ bhagavānnityadā yatra varṣati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śrā class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ śrā class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ śrā class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ śrā class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ śrā class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ śrā class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ śram class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ śram class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ śram class 4 verb]
[imperfect active second single], [aorist active second single]

translation: Being completely exhausted by (the strain of) running fast to such a long distance, they, however, ascended the lofty mountain peak known as Pravarṣaṇa where Indra the god of rain, always sends forth showers.

devanagari: गिरौ निलीनावाज्ञाय नाधिगम्य पदं नृप । ददाह गिरिमेधोभिः समन्तादग्निमुत्सृजन् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: girau nilīnāvājñāya nādhigamya padaṃ nṛpa dadāha girimedhobhiḥ samantādagnimutsṛjan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: Surmising that they must have concealed themselves somewhere on the mountain and not being able to locate their place of hiding, they (Jarāsandha’s men) set the whole mountain on fire on all sides with the fuel (the trees cut down by them during their search) strewn around.

devanagari: तत उत्पत्य तरसा दह्यमानतटादुभौ । दशैकयोजनात्तुङ्गान्निपेततुरधो भुवि ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tata utpatya tarasā dahyamānataṭādubhau daśaikayojanāttuṅgānnipetaturadho bhuvi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Thereupon, both Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma quickly (and with great velocity) leapt from the mountain peak which is eleven yojanas in height and had its sides burning, and descended on the plains below (beyond the army of Jarāsandha).

devanagari: अलक्ष्यमाणौ रिपुणा सानुगेन यदूत्तमौ । स्वपुरं पुनरायातौ समुद्र परिखां नृप ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: alakṣyamāṇau ripuṇā sānugena yadūttamau svapuraṃ punarāyātau samudra parikhāṃ nṛpa
translation: Without being noticed by enemy and his followers, those best sons of Yadu race returned to their capital Dvārakā which is surrounded by the sea as its moat, Oh king.

devanagari: सोऽपि दग्धाविति मृषा मन्वानो बलकेशवौ । बलमाकृष्य सुमहन्मगधान्मागधो ययौ ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: so balamākṛṣya sumahanmagadhānmāgadho yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The noble-souled Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma (the Mādhava brothers) entered the city of Dvārakā and lived happily in the company of their highly joyous relatives and were being praised day and night by bards and songsters like Sūta, Māgadha and Bandins.[4]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
End of chapter 55th in Padaratnāvalī’s Text
For ch. 56 in Padaratnāvalī’s Text vide the Appendix.

devanagari: आनर्ताधिपतिः श्रीमान्रैवतो रैवतीं सुताम् । ब्रह्मणा चोदितः प्रादाद्बलायेति पुरोदितम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: ānartādhipatiḥ śrīmānraivato raivatīṃ sutām brahmaṇā coditaḥ prādādbalāyeti puroditam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: It has been already narrated to you by me (vide Supra 9.3.27-36), that Kakudmin, the son of Revata, the celebrated king of Anarta gave his daughter Revatī in marriage to Balarāma, as per direction of god Brahmā.

devanagari: भगवानपि गोविन्द उपयेमे कुरूद्वह । वैदर्भीं भीष्मकसुतां श्रियो मात्रां स्वयंवरे ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavānapi govinda upayeme kurūdvaha vaidarbhīṃ bhīṣmakasutāṃ śriyo mātrāṃ svayaṃvare

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa also married Rukmiṇī, the princess of Viḍarbha and the daughter of Bhīṣmaka and who was an aṃśa (part-manifestation) of goddess Śrī in a svayaṃvara (‘selfchoice’ type of marriage where the girl elects of her own free will, her husband).

devanagari: प्रमथ्य तरसा राज्ञः शाल्वादींश्चैद्यपक्षगान् । पश्यतां सर्वलोकानां तार्क्ष्यपुत्रः सुधामिव ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: pramathya tarasā rājñaḥ śālvādīṃścaidyapakṣagān paśyatāṃ sarvalokānāṃ tārkṣyaputraḥ sudhāmiva
translation: Like Garuḍa, the son of Kaśyapa taking away the pitcher of nectar (defeating all the gods), Kṛṣṇa carried her away defeating instantly Śālva and other kings who were partisans and allies of Śiśupāla, the king of Cedis.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । भगवान्भीष्मकसुतां रुक्मिणीं रुचिराननाम् । राक्षसेन विधानेन उपयेम इति श्रुतम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca bhagavānbhīṣmakasutāṃ rukmiṇīṃ rucirānanām rākṣasena vidhānena upayema iti śrutam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[nominative single from √ śru class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ śru class 5 verb]

translation:
King Parīkṣit enquired:
It has been heard by us that the Lord married the beautiful looking Rukmiṇī, the princess of Bhīṣmaka as per rākṣasa form of marriage (wherein the girl is abducted per force in the teeth of opposition of relatives.)

devanagari: भगवन्श्रोतुमिच्छामि कृष्णस्यामिततेजसः । यथा मागधशाल्वादीन्जित्वा कन्यामुपाहरत् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavanśrotumicchāmi kṛṣṇasyāmitatejasaḥ yathā māgadhaśālvādīnjitvā kanyāmupāharat
translation: I desire to hear the story of Lord Kṛṣṇa of boundless power and energy, who defeated Jarāsandha, Śālva and others and carried away the girl, Oh worshipful sage.

devanagari: ब्रह्मन्कृष्णकथाः पुण्या माध्वीर्लोकमलापहाः । को नु तृप्येत शृण्वानः श्रुतज्ञो नित्यनूतनाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: brahmankṛṣṇakathāḥ puṇyā mādhvīrlokamalāpahāḥ ko nu tṛpyeta śṛṇvānaḥ śrutajño nityanūtanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ puṇ class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ puṇ class 10 verb]
[optative active second plural], [optative passive third single]

translation: Oh Brāhmaṇa sage! The stories of Lord Kṛṣṇa are meritorious, sweet, ever-interesting, delightful and they wipe out the sins of the world. What person who knows the importance of listening to them, will be sated at hearing them.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Here Vrindāvana edition gives the additional chapters 56 and a part or 57. These are translated in the Appendix.

devanagari: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाच । राजासीद्भीष्मको नाम विदर्भाधिपतिर्महान् । तस्य पञ्चाभवन्पुत्राः कन्यैका च वरानना ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībādarāyaṇiruvāca rājāsīdbhīṣmako nāma vidarbhādhipatirmahān tasya pañcābhavanputrāḥ kanyaikā ca varānanā

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka narrated:
There was a great king called Bhīṣmaka, a powerful monarch of the Vidarbhas. He had five sons and only one charming looking daughter.

devanagari: रुक्म्यग्रजो रुक्मरथो रुक्मबाहुरनन्तरः । रुक्मकेशो रुक्ममाली रुक्मिण्येषा स्वसा सती ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: rukmyagrajo rukmaratho rukmabāhuranantaraḥ rukmakeśo rukmamālī rukmiṇyeṣā svasā satī
translation: The eldest son was Rukmi. After him was born Rukmabāhu and then Rukmakeśa, Rukmamālin and the pious Rukmiṇī was their sister.

devanagari: सोपश्रुत्य मुकुन्दस्य रूपवीर्यगुणश्रियः । गृहागतैर्गीयमानास्तं मेने सदृशं पतिम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: sopaśrutya mukundasya rūpavīryaguṇaśriyaḥ gṛhāgatairgīyamānāstaṃ mene sadṛśaṃ patim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ man class 8 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Listening attentively to the glorification of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s beauty, prowess, excellent virtues and affluence as sung by the visitors to her palace, she regarded him as her worthy spouse.

devanagari: तां बुद्धिलक्षणौदार्य रूपशीलगुणाश्रयाम् । कृष्णश्च सदृशीं भार्यां समुद्वोढुं मनो दधे ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ buddhilakṣaṇaudārya rūpaśīlaguṇāśrayām kṛṣṇaśca sadṛśīṃ bhāryāṃ samudvoḍhuṃ mano dadhe

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Kṛṣṇa also made up his mind to marry her as he knew her to be a suitable wife due to her intelligence, auspicious characteristics, nobility of heart, beauty, high character and other excellences.

devanagari: बन्धूनामिच्छतां दातुं कृष्णाय भगिनीं नृप । ततो निवार्य कृष्णद्विड्रुक्मी चैद्यममन्यत ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: bandhūnāmicchatāṃ dātuṃ kṛṣṇāya bhaginīṃ nṛpa tato nivārya kṛṣṇadviḍrukmī caidyamamanyata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[imperfect middle third single], [imperfect passive third single]

translation: In spite of all brothers being desirous of giving their sister in marriage to Kṛṣṇa, Rukmi who hated Kṛṣṇa, came in the way of it and proposed Śiśupāla, the king of Cedis.

devanagari: तदवेत्यासितापाङ्गी वैदर्भी दुर्मना भृशम् । विचिन्त्याप्तं द्विजं कञ्चित्कृष्णाय प्राहिणोद्द्रुतम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tadavetyāsitāpāṅgī vaidarbhī durmanā bhṛśam vicintyāptaṃ dvijaṃ kañcitkṛṣṇāya prāhiṇoddrutam
translation: Grasping the situation, the beautiful princess of Vidarbha was deeply agitated at heart. She pondered over the problem and immediately despatched a trusted Brāhmaṇa to Kṛṣṇa with the mission of bringing him.

devanagari: द्वारकां स समभ्येत्य प्रतीहारैः प्रवेशितः । अपश्यदाद्यं पुरुषमासीनं काञ्चनासने ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: dvārakāṃ sa samabhyetya pratīhāraiḥ praveśitaḥ apaśyadādyaṃ puruṣamāsīnaṃ kāñcanāsane

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ad ], [accusative single from √ ad ]

translation: Arriving at Dvārakā, he was ushered into (Kṛṣṇa’s presence) by the door-attendants where he saw the First (the most ancient) Person (in the world) seated on a throne of gold.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा ब्रह्मण्यदेवस्तमवरुह्य निजासनात् । उपवेश्यार्हयां चक्रे यथात्मानं दिवौकसः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā brahmaṇyadevastamavaruhya nijāsanāt upaveśyārhayāṃ cakre yathātmānaṃ divaukasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Seeing him come, Lord Kṛṣṇa who was always friendly to Brāhmaṇas, got down from his throne. Offering a (respectable) seat to the Brāhmaṇa, he worshipped him (as an honourable guest) as gods used to treat him.

devanagari: तं भुक्तवन्तं विश्रान्तमुपगम्य सतां गतिः । पाणिनाभिमृशन्पादावव्यग्रस्तमपृच्छत ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ bhuktavantaṃ viśrāntamupagamya satāṃ gatiḥ pāṇinābhimṛśanpādāvavyagrastamapṛcchata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ mṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛś class 6 verb]

translation: When he had taken his meals and rested comfortably, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the final asylum of the saintly people, approached him and massaging gently his feet with his hand, he quietly enquired of the Brāhmaṇa.

devanagari: कच्चिद्द्विजवरश्रेष्ठ धर्मस्ते वृद्धसम्मतः । वर्तते नातिकृच्छ्रेण सन्तुष्टमनसः सदा ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: kacciddvijavaraśreṣṭha dharmaste vṛddhasammataḥ vartate nātikṛcchreṇa santuṣṭamanasaḥ sadā
translation: “Oh most prominent one among great Brāhmaṇas! Is your righteous course of conduct traditionally approved by the elders, going on without any difficulty? (I hope) you are (happy with it and) contented in your heart.

devanagari: सन्तुष्टो यर्हि वर्तेत ब्राह्मणो येन केनचित् । अहीयमानः स्वद्धर्मात्स ह्यस्याखिलकामधुक् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: santuṣṭo yarhi varteta brāhmaṇo yena kenacit ahīyamānaḥ svaddharmātsa hyasyākhilakāmadhuk

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: If a Brāhmaṇa adjusts himself in contentment, with whatever he gets by chance, but does not swerve from his course of righteousness, he is like a wish-yielding cow capable of granting blessings to all the world (or that righteous course of conduct serves as a wish-yielding cow to him)

devanagari: असन्तुष्टोऽसकृल्लोकानाप्नोत्यपि सुरेश्वरः । अकिञ्चनोऽपि सन्तुष्टः शेते सर्वाङ्गविज्वरः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: asantuṣṭo akiñcano
translation: Even Indra, the king of gods, if discontented, has to wander from one world to another restlessly.[6] But a destitute person, if contented, sleeps soundly without a touch of feverish anxiety to his body and mind.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
v.l. nāpnoti: Even though he is the lord of gods he does not attain to higher worlds but suffers—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: विप्रान्स्वलाभसन्तुष्टान्साधून्भूतसुहृत्तमान् । निरहङ्कारिणः शान्तान्नमस्ये शिरसा सकृत् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: viprānsvalābhasantuṣṭānsādhūnbhūtasuhṛttamān nirahaṅkāriṇaḥ śāntānnamasye śirasā sakṛt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]
[imperfect middle first single], [imperfect passive first single]

translation: I bow down again and again with my head to those Brāhmaṇas who are happy with the realization of their self[7], pious and the best friends and well-wishers of all beings, free from ego, serene and self complacent.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Or: With what they get as a result of their destiny or karmas.

devanagari: कच्चिद्वः कुशलं ब्रह्मन्राजतो यस्य हि प्रजाः । सुखं वसन्ति विषये पाल्यमानाः स मे प्रियः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccidvaḥ kuśalaṃ brahmanrājato yasya hi prajāḥ sukhaṃ vasanti viṣaye pālyamānāḥ sa me priyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vas class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vas class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vas class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ pāl class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ pāl class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ pāl class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ pāl class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ pāl class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ pā ], [vocative plural from √ pā ]
[nominative plural from √ pā ], [vocative plural from √ pā ], [accusative plural from √ pā ]

translation: Are you all happy (with a feeling of security) under your ruler, Oh Brāhmaṇa? That king is liked by me in whose country the subjects protected by him lead a happy life.

devanagari: यतस्त्वमागतो दुर्गं निस्तीर्येह यदिच्छया । सर्वं नो ब्रूह्यगुह्यं चेत्किं कार्यं करवाम ते ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: yatastvamāgato durgaṃ nistīryeha yadicchayā sarvaṃ no brūhyaguhyaṃ cetkiṃ kāryaṃ karavāma te

[nominative single from √ kṝ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṝ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṝ class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ kṝ class 9 verb]

translation: Please tell me the place from which you have come here crossing this (unforḍable) sea and the object of seeing me. If it be no undivulgable secret, please tell us everything—what we should do for you?”

devanagari: एवं सम्पृष्टसम्प्रश्नो ब्राह्मणः परमेष्ठिना । लीलागृहीतदेहेन तस्मै सर्वमवर्णयत् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sampṛṣṭasampraśno brāhmaṇaḥ parameṣṭhinā līlāgṛhītadehena tasmai sarvamavarṇayat
translation: Placing the letter (written by Rukmiṇī) at his feet, he bowed down to him. The Lord of the world, with beautiful smiling eyes, took it up. The Lord, Dharma incarnate, read it attentively as if he was eagerly desirous to read it.

devanagari: श्रीरुक्मिण्युवाच । श्रुत्वा गुणान्भुवनसुन्दर शृण्वतां ते । निर्विश्य कर्णविवरैर्हरतोऽङ्गतापम् । रूपं दृशां दृशिमतामखिलार्थलाभं । त्वय...
english_devnagari: śrīrukmiṇyuvāca śrutvā guṇānbhuvanasundara śṛṇvatāṃ te nirviśya karṇavivarairharato rūpaṃ dṛśāṃ dṛśimatāmakhilārthalābhaṃ tvayyacyutāviśati cittamapatrapaṃ me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[accusative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation:
Rukmiṇī said in her message:
Oh the most-beautiful Person in all the worlds! Having listened to your excellent qualities which enter into the heart through (the passage of) ears and remove all the feverishness of the body and the mind, and of your excellent charms ensuring all the cherished blessings to those who possess eyes, my heart sheds all bashfulness and enters in you, Oh Immortal Lord!

devanagari: का त्वा मुकुन्द महती कुलशीलरूप । विद्यावयोद्रविणधामभिरात्मतुल्यम् । धीरा पतिं कुलवती न वृणीत कन्या । काले नृसिंह नरलोकमनोऽभिरामम् ॥ ३८...
english_devnagari: kā tvā mukunda mahatī kulaśīlarūpa vidyāvayodraviṇadhāmabhirātmatulyam dhīrā patiṃ kulavatī na vṛṇīta kanyā kāle nṛsiṃha naralokamano

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tul class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ tul class 10 verb]
[optative middle third single], [imperative active second plural]
[optative middle third single], [imperative active second plural]

translation: You are the glorious consort of goddess Lakṣmī, loved by Your devotees, the Supreme Controller cf the Universe who are resorted to for protection by helpless people and as such also by me. Taking this into account, be pleased to grant the prayer of mine who am a servant of your lotus-like feet. Oh dearest Lord I have submitted myself to your feet.

devanagari: तन्मे भवान्खलु वृतः पतिरङ्ग जायाम् । आत्मार्पितश्च भवतोऽत्र विभो विधेहि । मा वीरभागमभिमर्शतु चैद्य आराद् । गोमायुवन्मृगपतेर्बलिमम्बुजा...
english_devnagari: tanme bhavānkhalu vṛtaḥ patiraṅga jāyām ātmārpitaśca bhavato mā vīrabhāgamabhimarśatu caidya ārād gomāyuvanmṛgapaterbalimambujākṣa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: I have, therefore, chosen you as my lord indeed. Oh Lotus-eyed dear lord! I have submitted my self unto you. Please do take me as your wife. Like a jackal polluting by his touch the lion’s share of the prey, may not Śiśupāla, the king of Cedis come hastily and touch the hero’s share (i.e. myself).

devanagari: पूर्तेष्टदत्तनियमव्रतदेवविप्र । गुर्वर्चनादिभिरलं भगवान्परेशः । आराधितो यदि गदाग्रज एत्य पाणिं । गृह्णातु मे न दमघोषसुतादयोऽन्ये ॥ ४० ...
english_devnagari: pūrteṣṭadattaniyamavratadevavipra gurvarcanādibhiralaṃ bhagavānpareśaḥ ārādhito yadi gadāgraja etya pāṇiṃ gṛhṇātu me na damaghoṣasutādayo
translation: If the Supreme Lord, the Almighty, has been properly propitiated by me by religious deeds such as digging wells, performing sacrifices, giving donations, observance of religious rules and vows and the worship of gods, Brāhmaṇas and preceptors and such other deeds, may Lord Kṛṣṇa, the elder brother of Gada, come and marry me so that others like the son of Damaghoṣa (Śiśupāla) may not take my hands.

devanagari: श्वो भाविनि त्वमजितोद्वहने विदर्भान् । गुप्तः समेत्य पृतनापतिभिः परीतः । निर्मथ्य चैद्यमगधेन्द्र बलं प्रसह्य । मां राक्षसेन विधिनोद्वह...
english_devnagari: śvo bhāvini tvamajitodvahane vidarbhān guptaḥ sametya pṛtanāpatibhiḥ parītaḥ nirmathya caidyamagadhendra balaṃ prasahya māṃ rākṣasena vidhinodvaha vīryaśulkām
translation: Oh Invincible Lord! May you come secretly with an army and generals to Vidarbha at the time of marriage which is to be celebrated very shortly. Defeat the armies of Caidya and Jarāsandha, the king of Magadha, and take me away as the spoil of your prowess according to the Rākṣasa form of marriage.[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
This form of marriage is recommended for Kṣatriyas.

devanagari: अन्तःपुरान्तरचरीमनिहत्य बन्धून् । त्वामुद्वहे कथमिति प्रवदाम्युपायम् । पूर्वेद्युरस्ति महती कुलदेवयात्रा । यस्यां बहिर्नववधूर्गिरिजामु...
english_devnagari: antaḥpurāntaracarīmanihatya bandhūn tvāmudvahe kathamiti pravadāmyupāyam pūrvedyurasti mahatī kuladevayātrā yasyāṃ bahirnavavadhūrgirijāmupeyāt
translation: 1 Coming over there at that time with the four divisions of army such as chariots, elephants, cavalry and infantry, and having given a sound drubbing to kings of Śālva, Magadha, Cedi and others, may the: Lord of Lakṣmī take me along with my maids of honour from the temple to his own capital and have there a formal marriage celebration (lit. taking of hands).

devanagari: यस्याङ्घ्रिपङ्कजरजःस्नपनं महान्तो । वाञ्छन्त्युमापतिरिवात्मतमोऽपहत्यै । यर्ह्यम्बुजाक्ष न लभेय भवत्प्रसादं । जह्यामसून्व्रतकृशान्शतजन्...
english_devnagari: yasyāṅghripaṅkajarajaḥsnapanaṃ mahānto vāñchantyumāpatirivātmatamo yarhyambujākṣa na labheya bhavatprasādaṃ jahyāmasūnvratakṛśānśatajanmabhiḥ syāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vāñch class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ vāñch class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vāñch class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vāñch class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vāñch class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vāñch class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ vāñch class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vāñch class 1 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
1 Pondering over all the contents of the letter as well as the oral message of the princess, Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of demon Madhu, looked at the Brāhmaṇa (to convey his message).

devanagari: ब्राह्मण उवाच । इत्येते गुह्यसन्देशा यदुदेव मया हृताः । विमृश्य कर्तुं यच्चात्र क्रियतां तदनन्तरम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇa uvāca ityete guhyasandeśā yadudeva mayā hṛtāḥ vimṛśya kartuṃ yaccātra kriyatāṃ tadanantaram

[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single], [imperative passive third single]

translation:
The Brāhmaṇa said:
Oh Lord of Yadus! These are the secret words of the message that I have bought for you. Please consider what is worth doing in this (exigency of the situation), and prompt action should be taken immediately after this.
[back to top]

devanagari: अथ द्विपञ्चाशत्तमोऽध्यायः 10. ॥ 52 ॥
english_devnagari: atha dvipañcāśattamo
